params.section: Earners' levy deducted on earnings up to an annually prescribed maximum

params.anchor: NZD $20,000 for each year, if you are studying for 1 year or more, or

params.section: Any person, whatever their age, who is employed by an employer to do any work for hire or reward under a ‘contract of service’ (commonly called an ‘employment agreement’).

params.anchor: The eligibility age will increase from 18–30 to 18–35.
Most youth will also have the option to participate twice in the program—each time for up to 24 months.

rowLabels: Your applicationThis amount includes the processing fee and the right of permanent residence fee.

params.section: Find out if you need to give biometrics.

headers: For the part of the taxpayer's working holiday taxable income that:

livePolicy.reason: The official 2025 Act PDF uses compressed PDF features outside the restricted parser; an authorised human review is required.

params.anchor: The rate of levy payable by a person upon a taxable income is 2%.

headers: Super guarantee (%) for Norfolk Island (transitional rate) (from 1 July 2016)

livePolicy.reason: The official ATO content representation is served as a PDF to browsers and rejects unattended HTML requests; the restricted verifier cannot safely extract it.

items: $37,500 or less, you will get the maximum offset of $700
between $37,501 and $45,000, you will get $700 minus 5 cents for every $1 above $37,500
between $45,001 and $66,667, you will get $325 minus 1.5 cents for every $1 above $45,000.

params.anchor: From 1 July 2026, passport holders from Cyprus, Finland, Germany, and Republic of Korea passport holders are eligible to apply for a Working Holiday (subclass 417) visa if they are 18 to 35 years of age (inclusive).

match.visaSubclassText: Employer Nomination Scheme visa (subclass 186) - Temporary Residence Transition stream
